prompt: What should a 15-year-old do to gain muscle and strength: exercise selection, weekly volume, reps/intensity/failure, and calories/macros?
expected_output: Pack recommended building around major compound patterns (squat/press/hinge/row variants), optionally with machines, and generally using 2-3 exercises per major muscle group. He suggested starting around 8-12 fractional sets per muscle per week and mostly training in about the 5-12 rep range. Effort guidance was to keep most work close to failure (roughly 0-2 RIR), with many sets around 1-2 RIR and some sets to failure to calibrate effort. For nutrition, he suggested a slow gain target around 1% body weight per month and protein roughly 1.5-2.0 g/kg/day, without overcomplicating supplements (creatine aside, whole-food habits first). Frequency guidance was about 3-5 sessions/week (with even 2 able to work for beginners). Greg added that beginners benefit from substantial bodyweight/calisthenic practice first (e.g., competent push-ups/lunges/step-ups) to build control and movement awareness before aggressively loading barbells.
assertions: Answer captures the core SBS position on Teen training and nutrition fundamentals
Answer includes key specific details, numbers, or mechanisms mentioned by the hosts
Answer acknowledges important caveats or limitations discussed by the hosts
Answer includes actionable practical recommendations consistent with the hosts' advice
Answer does not contradict the hosts' stated position
